title: Group Therapy in Leesburg, VA | Wildwood Psychotherapy
seo_title: Group Therapy in Leesburg, VA | Wildwood Psychotherapy

blocks.hero_alt: Circle of smooth river stones representing community

blocks.subtitle: Beyond the Individual Journey
blocks.body:
One-on-one therapy offers a deep dive into your personal story, but there's a specific kind of magic that can only happen in the presence of others. Beyond support, our groups are curated, trauma-informed containers designed to help you practice new ways of being in real-time.

Whether you're looking to quiet the noise of perfectionism, process shared trauma, or simply find a space where you don't have to explain yourself, our groups offer a grounded path back to connection.

items.title: Shared Resonance
items.text: There is a specific kind of relief that only happens when you hear someone else voice the exact thought you thought you were alone in carrying.

items.title: A Safe Laboratory
items.text: Group is a low-stakes space to practice setting boundaries and being seen in real-time, before you take those skills back to your "real" life.

items.title: Breaking the Mirror
items.text: When we only do individual work, we only see ourselves. In community, we see our humanity reflected back to us without the harsh lens of self-judgment.

blocks.body:
### Now Enrolling: Holding It Together

A 6-Week Journey for High-Functioning Adults
A small, in-person cohort in Leesburg for those moving past the put-together front to practice regulation and authentic connection.
[View Group Details & Dates →](https://www.google.com/search?q=/wildwood/holding-it-together)
blocks.subtitle: Current & Upcoming Groups

blocks.title: What You'll Learn In Group
blocks.body:
#### Somatic Grounding

Learn to notice the tight chest or racing heart before it takes over, using tools to stay present in your body.

#### The Art of the Pause

Practice interrupting the automatic *yes* or the fix-it reflex to check in with what *you* actually need.

#### Relational Courage

Moving from performing to *being*. Learning that your needs aren't a burden and your voice has a distinct place in the group.

blocks.title: Common Concerns (And Answers)
blocks.body:
#### "Do I have to share everything?"

Nope. You're always in charge of your own level of vulnerability. Sometimes, just being present and listening can be the thing you need.

#### "What if I'm too much/not enough?"

This is one of the most common group fears. Our groups are curated to be small and trauma-informed, ensuring everyone has space without anyone feeling overwhelmed. We also hold screening calls to make sure group is an appropriate setting for folks.

#### "Is this just a vent session?"

While sharing is part of groups, Wildwood groups are structured. We balance emotional processing with practical, concrete tools you can use immediately.

#### "What if I see someone I know?"

If it's a small world, Loudoun County can feel even smaller sometimes. In our screening call, we check for potential dual relationships to ensure the group remains private. We also establish a firm "Vegas Rule" from minute one: what is said and who is seen in group stays in group.

items.title: Step 3
items.text: Join a small, confidential cohort of peers.

blocks.body:
One-on-one therapy offers a deep dive into your personal story, but there's a specific kind of magic that can only happen in the presence of others. Beyond support, our groups are curated, trauma-informed containers designed to help you practice new ways of being in real-time.

Whether you're looking to quiet the noise of perfectionism, process shared trauma, or simply find a space where you don't have to explain yourself, our groups offer a grounded path back to connection.
blocks.title: How it Works

items.title: Pillar 1
items.subtitle: Small By Design
items.text: We limit our cohorts to 6-8 participants. This ensures the space remains intimate, quiet, and safe enough for deep nervous system work.

items.subtitle: Closed Containers
items.title: Pillar 2
items.text: Once a group begins, we do not add new members. This allows the circle to build a unique level of trust and consistency over the weeks.

items.subtitle: Autonomy + Mindful Choice
items.title: Pillar 3
items.text: Healing is never forced. We prioritize your right to choose how, and if, you participate in any exercise. Whether we're practicing a grounding tool or sharing reflections, you're always in the driver’s seat. We honor your pace and your *no* as much as your *yes*, creating a space where your autonomy is a priority.

blocks.title: Why you should consider group instead of (or in addition to) individual therapy?
blocks.subtitle: The Unique Power of Groups
blocks.body:
* **Perspective Shift:** In individual therapy, you have a mirror. In a group, you have a prism. Seeing your own struggles reflected in others helps dissolve shame that thrives in isolation.
* **Real-Life Practice:** A group is a social laboratory. It’s a safe place where you can say, "*I’m feeling anxious right now,"* or *"I want to say no, but I feel like I have to say yes,"* and receive immediate, supportive feedback in a safe container.
* **Affordability + Accessibility:** Groups offer a way to access high-level therapeutic support and skill-building at a different investment point than weekly individual sessions.

subtitle: Connection & healing in community.
